name: Cariño - veulta de Costa Ártabra
region_intro: Mountain biking around Cariño offers routes through a landscape defined by its rugged Atlantic coastline and dramatic natural features. The region features the Sierra de A Capelada mountain range, home to high oceanic cliffs and expansive meadows. Trails often traverse varied terrain, including coastal paths and inland routes with significant elevation changes. This area provides a challenging environment for mountain bikers seeking diverse experiences. Best mountain bike trails around Cariño The most popular mountain bike trail is Cariño - veulta de Costa Ártabra, a difficult 34.3 miles (55.2 km) trail that takes 5 hours 17 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain and coastal views. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Medieval defense tower – O Porto do Barqueiro loop from Ortigueira, a moderate 31.0 miles (49.9 km) path. This loop offers a mix of terrain, passing historical landmarks and scenic coastal areas. Local mountain bikers also love the Rest area loop from Ortigueira, a 27.0 miles (43.4 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 50 minutes. Mountain biking around Cariño is defined by rugged Atlantic coastline, high oceanic cliffs, and mountain ranges. The network offers options for both moderate and difficult ability levels. The routes in Cariño are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. More than 70 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Cariño's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Cariño?
A: There are 19 mountain bike trails available around Cariño, offering a range of experiences for different skill levels. These include 13 moderate routes and 6 difficult routes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Cariño?
A: Mountain bike trails in Cariño are characterized by a rugged Atlantic coastline, high oceanic cliffs, and varied mountain ranges. You'll encounter diverse terrain, from coastal paths with dramatic views to inland routes with significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Cariño offers several challenging routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e982899092/carino-veulta-de-costa-artabra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cariño - veulta de Costa Ártabra&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 55.2 km trail with over 1500 meters of elevation gain, known for its coastal views and demanding sections. Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38176249" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of O Porto de Bares – O Porto do Barqueiro loop from Ortigueira&lt;/a&gt;, covering 53.4 km with nearly 1000 meters of ascent.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails often pass through the dramatic landscapes of the Sierra de A Capelada, offering views of some of Europe's highest oceanic cliffs. You can also encounter significant landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3479074" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cape Ortegal Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt; and various viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/425142" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Teixidelo Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/425141" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garita Herbeira (611m)&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes may also lead you near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3464641" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casón Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that are loop trails?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are designed as loops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31892711" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medieval defense tower – O Porto do Barqueiro loop from Ortigueira&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 49.9 km loop that combines historical landmarks with scenic coastal areas. Another loop option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38177017" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rest area loop from Ortigueira&lt;/a&gt;, a 43.4 km trail through varied landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Cariño?
A: The region's Atlantic climate means conditions can vary. Generally, spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and fewer crowds, making them ideal for exploring. Summer can also be good, but be prepared for potentially warmer weather and more visitors. Winter riding is possible, but trails might be wetter and windier, especially along the exposed coastline.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Cariño?
A: While many routes in Cariño feature significant elevation and rugged terrain, there are 13 moderate trails that could be suitable for families with some mountain biking experience. It's recommended to check the specific route details and elevation profiles on komoot to ensure it matches your family's skill level and endurance.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails?
A: Many outdoor trails in Galicia are dog-friendly, but it's always best to check local regulations or specific route descriptions on komoot for any restrictions, especially in protected natural areas or private lands. Ensure your dog is well-behaved, on a leash where required, and that you carry waste bags.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain bike trails in Cariño?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. Many routes start from or pass through towns like Cariño or Ortigueira, where public parking is generally available. For specific trailheads, it's advisable to check the komoot route details or local maps for designated parking areas.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Cariño?
A: The mountain bike trails around Cariño are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. Riders often praise the dramatic coastal scenery, the challenging terrain, and the sense of adventure offered by the rugged Atlantic landscape.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that offer views of the Loiba cliffs?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20634638" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loiba cliffs – O Picón Tower loop from Ortigueira&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 49.7 km route that will take you past the stunning Loiba cliffs, offering spectacular views of the coastline and the Atlantic Ocean.

Q: Are there cultural or historical points of interest near the mountain biking routes?
A: Yes, beyond the natural beauty, the region has cultural significance. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3157808" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint Andrew of Teixido hermitage&lt;/a&gt;, located at the foot of the Sierra de A Capelada, is a notable historical and cultural site that can be visited in conjunction with your mountain biking adventures.
tips: Coastal path above the costa del morte, where the land meets the sea, creating sharp edges and breathtaking cliffs

Part of the coastal path that joins Cedeira with Cariño, crossing the impressive Sierra da Capelada, with spectacular views. Chao do Monte, located 360 meters above sea level, below you can see the rocky and inaccessible Playa de Cortés
Trail trail to the Casón jump and the Pozo do Inferno that leads to the Casón Castle viewpoint or the Ortigueira estuary
